title: Level 66
wikitext:
{{Rewrite|SPaG and tone errors.|date=10/06/2026 (June)}} {{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 66: "Active Construction"}} {{BackgroundFitted|file=generated2.jpg}} {{Image Banner|image=missingtexture.jpg|caption=There will always be more behind the next door.}} {{Alt Class|no=66|ext=4|env=3|ent=0}} '''Level 66''' is the 67th level of the Backrooms. =='''Description'''==
[[File:{{{file|generated1.png}}}|{{{width|300px}}}]]
{{{caption|A photo of a long-generated tunnel that spans for miles. Photographer status dead.}}}
{{Blurimage|file=generated1.png|caption=|align=center}}
Level 66, commonly known as "Active Construction," possesses no stable layout, and attempts to map it have consistently failed due to its ever-changing structure. Level 66 is characterized by architecture that appears to construct itself in real time. Although each separate environment may be entirely unique from visit to visit, one thing remains consistent for all reports - the architecture around changes as the wanderer travels through it. The entrance to Level 66 always starts in what is called an Entrance Hall, which is essentially a near-identical corridor. This section of the level features brown painted walls, carpets on the floor, as well as several wall scones emitting a dim amber light. Up to five or fifteen doors can be seen throughout the hall, depending on the individual experience of entering it. A person will usually detect something amiss about the area beyond a portal opened in Level 66. In one instance, witnesses heard sounds of metal being impacted along with the sound of machinery working and structures moving prior to the appearance of a new room. Another report mentions the emergence of fragments of walls, floors, and ceiling material from thin air before forming into actual rooms. The process takes no more than a few seconds after which the constructure environment is solid and stable. The areas produced by this anomaly tend to lack cohesion. A modern office space might be followed by an abandoned maintence tunnel, while a residential hallway might lead directly into an industrial area, and rooms complete with furniture could be followed by completely empty concrete areas. It has been noted that although closer inspection usually detects some strange features like illogical proportions and unnecessary objects. It seems that the level spawns architecture soley on the occasions when it becomes important to the progress of the wanderer. Unexplored areas which had been left alone for some times were discovered to be changed or even substituted the next time they were visited. Nevertheless, the level is locally coherent with its architecture generally being geometrically correct. If there is any conflict between structure, then the conflicting doors become locked permanently. Exit signs can be encountered all around the level. But so far there have been no discoveries made regarding finding an exit according to their location. In fact, quite a few signs just point to dead ends, unfinished passages. or even unfinished rooms which are seemingly empty as if they had never been completed. No two explorations seem to be identical although several locations seem to be repeated in different explorations. ==='''Room Generation'''=== {{Blurimage|file=generated2.jpg|caption=A rare photo that shows procedural generation in real time. Photographer status alive.|align=center}} It seems that the environment in Level 66 is constructed without regard to any kind of pattern by some unknown means. The architectural style, purpose of the building, and environmental settings often vary drastically when moving from one place to another. For example, a fully equipped office might lead directly into a concrete pipe, and a residential hallway might suddenly open into a warehouse, hotel hallway, or maintence facility. To date, no reliable way of predicting what awaits behind closed doors has been found. There have been reports indicating that new territories become established only once they have been accessed. Wanderers claim to hear various construction noises from behind the door prior to opening it, such as metal clashes, grinding of machinery, and the movement of massive objects. There were even accounts of partially constructed walls, ceilings and furniture becoming visible for up to several seconds prior to their full manifestation. The types of items found inside generated environments differ as widely as the designs of the spaces themselves. While some environments hold nothing more than an absence of items worth noting, other environments might contain furniture, electronics, stored supplies, or large amounts of resources needed to survive. Although the generation seems to be completely artificial, all of the items that can be found within a generated environment seem to function normally. However, there is absolutely no correlation between the design of a space and the number of resources that can be found inside of it. Sometimes, a generated environment turns out to be dangerous, with rooms being either on fire, flooded with water, filled with chemical, or at the point of collapse. Yet, even with these dangers, such environments have unusual limitations in regard to their containment. The water remains only side the flooded rooms, smoke never leaves burning chambers, and chemicals stay within the environments in which they were generated. Although most of the places that are generated seem like levels one may see throughout the Backrooms, there are a few unique rooms and corridors that seem to recur in many different explorations carried out by wanderers who have had their own separate exploration experience of the level. The same layouts, furniture, and designs of these places make one think that the Level 66 consists of a limited number of pre0stablished blueprints. ----
  • Template Instance 04
  • Storage Annex
  • The Outlook
  • Rendering Errors
  • Excised Areas
----
{{Blurimage|file=Level_Time.jpg|caption=First ever photo of Template Instance 04. Photographer status unknown.|align=center}} Template Instance 04 is among the most frequently observed recurring locations in Level 66. The environment involves a long corridor flanked by walls that are uniform and of cream color. There are also wall lights installed along the walls with residential-style windows. From a distance, the corridors seem normal; however, upon closer inspection, many architectural abnormalities can be noticed. The corridors feature repetitive units of architecture that seems to repeat in succession without much deviation. Windows, support walls, wall lights, and doorways seem to repeat in regular intervals, giving the impression that a certain section of architecture has been cloned rather than created naturally. Many lost wanderers comment on how the corridors looks like an incomplete blueprint that was stretched beyond the required extent. Despite the fact the corridor appears architecturally complete, it exhibits certain inconsistencies. The length of the corridor varies depending on how it is measured, and many people who attempt to count architectural features end counting different numbers even while being inside the same space. Sometimes, sections of the hallway seem to shift positions, resulting in marked positions getting lost. As a result of the striking similarity between the encounters, M.E.G. has classified the environment as Template Instance 04, believing that it is one of the designs that the map uses to create the level. ----
----
{{Blurimage|file=storageannex.jpg|caption=A photo of the storage annex found within Level 66. Photographer status lost.|align=center}} Storage Annex is a location that is marked by its extended corridors filled with numerous storage containers that stretch beyond the player's view. The walls of this area consist of corrugated metal sheets, while the overhead pipes and ventilation systems are made of metal. Fluorescent lighting, which is accompanied by a monotonous sound, provides a illumination in this area. Unlike other locations in Level 66, the Storage Annex exhibits an unusally high level of structural uniformity. The hallways locate in this area are generally organized into a grid system and cross each other at regular intervals, creating a set of corridors that look extremely similar, attempts at mapping the area have been unsuccessful as the area constantly changes each revisit. All the units are normally locked, yet there are some units that can be seen partly open. What can be seen inside the units differs greatly; from completely empty units to units packed with furniture office equipment building material, domestic items and a few other random objects. Some rare occasions involve units containing objects that seem to be freshly generated objects. These objects seem unfinished, lack certain pieces, or seem to be constructed improperly. It is worth noting that the storage annex has an exceptional connection with the level's exit signs. The exit signs can always be seen hanging around the corridors and tend to point towards the more remote areas of the annex. Despite being abundant, there has not been one sign that led wanderers out of Level 66. On the contrary, they end up pointing towards locked units, dead ends or incomplete structures. Researchers theorize that the Storage Annex could be a storage place for unused architectural materials and components used to facilitate Level 66's generation process. While this theory still lacks confirmation, it might clarify why there is always some unfinished work there and what links the Storage Annex to the process of Level 66's creation. ----
----
{{Blurimage|file=outlook1.jpg|caption=A photo of The Outlook. Photographer status alive.|align=center}} The Outlook is an location that repeats itself and is characterized by a bright corridor that runs through it, filled with stainless-steel countertops, sinks, and windows, which look out into the open white space beyond. The unusual feeling of calmness is what characterizes this environment and what make it stand alone from most other locations found within Level 66. The atmosphere in The Outlook has been described as soothing. Most of the wanderer feel an instant sense of relief after entering the area. Heart rates that were previously elevated usually get back to normal quickly, and the feeling of tiredness diminishes rapidly. A few wanderers have even spent days inside The Outlook without succumbing to any of the paranoia and confusion felt when exploring the Backrooms. All efforts in reaching the exterior have been futile. It is impossible to break, damage, or remove the windows. In addition, there is no trace of any entrance door or other way out found. It appears from the analysis that there might not be any real landscape on the other side of the windowpane. It becomes increasingly hard to block out the landscape seen through the windows the longer one spends in The Outlook. Wanderers frequently end up spending a great deal of time observing the horizon without knowing how much time has gone by. Some say that the outside landscape is no longer a natural landscape, but one built to look like the nature itself. The clouds seen above the landscape never seen to move, nor does any weather occur. Another strange characteristic of The Outlook is its immunity to change. Objects left inside this environment seem to persist for years, and any marking made on the walls and counter surfaces have been found years later after whoever created them disappeared. In contrast to the other rooms in Level 66, which keep generating, changing, or being completely substituted, The Outlook seems strangely stable. This anomaly has led some experts to believe that The Outlook was created not just as another room, but as a fixed element in order for Level 66 to orient itself. ----
----
{{Blurimage|file=missingtexture.jpg|caption=A rare photo of a Missing Texture after Level 66 fails to generate new rooms. Photographer status dead.|align=center}} Level 66 generally produces cohesive environments but sometimes the creation process does not seem to work correctly, thus producing anomalies known as Rendering Errors, which are environments showing signs of partially built. In Contrast to ordinary rooms, Rendering Errors usually do not correspond to the architectural design seen elsewhere in the level. The worst possible Rendering Errors appears as massive checkerboards consisting of alternating squares of black and bright magenta colors. This effect is often seen on walls, floors, ceilings, furniture, and even entire rooms. Unlike painted surfaces, the checkerboard effect does not depend on distance, lighting or persepective - it is always perfect. Efforts aimed at altering or destroying the pattern have proved ineffective. Locations affected by Rendering Errors often display irregularities. Sound can get distorted or muffled, while shadows can vanish from view. The architecture surrounding the affected locations often has other sign of incomplete construction. The checkerboard pattern is shown to slowly extend onto the surrounding structures for several hours before disappearing once Level 66's finalizes its architecture. According to M.E.G. researchers, such locations are areas where Level 66 was unable to finalize the process of generating the area in question. However, there is no evidence of the pattern eventually transforming into any sort of construction. It is thought that Rendering Errors also happen because Level 66 produces its environments too quickly for it to build them proper. It is not clear whether or not these errors are glitches or simply part of the level's process of generating itself. ----
----
{{Blurimage|file=deleted1.jpg|caption=A common photo of an Excised Area found within Level 66. Photographer status alive.|align=center}} Excised Areas are a form of Generation Anomalies that is thought to happen due to Level 66 deleting portions of its own construction. Such areas may include unfinished structures, closed doors with no means of entry, dead-end hallways. and anything else suggesting that some portion of the level was previously constructed before being deliberately deleted. One of the most common occurrences of this type is that of the standalone door. Standalone doors can be seen hanging off a suspended platform. blocked off by a staircase that should have provided access to it, or even above the area on some form of structure that would not have allowed normal access. Often, pieces of railing or hallways suggest where this deletion occurred. Excised Areas do not behave like Rendering Errors; instead, they are more systematic and well-formed. In contrast to Rendering Errors, there is no new, incomplete geometry replacing what is missing in the surroundings. Rather, the area appears as though there was never any architecture there at all, with walls abruptly ending, floors ending, and beams continuing into thin air. There have been some theories put forward which proposes that Level 66 can modify itself. This would mean that Level 66 constantly monitor itself and deletes the parts that it finds unfavorable or unnecessary. One possible theory proposes that these deletions are not accidental at all. Multiple wanderers claimed to have discovered Excised Areas by observing strange architectural designs, repeated motifs, and doors that seemed increasingly irrelevant against the background of the environment around them. Some wanderers even believe that the level deletes certain paths leading to exits. In favor of this theory, one can refer to the fact that majority of Excised Areas feature in accessible doors. In addition to completely unreachable entrances, some wanderers were lucky enough to gain access to these areas through various architectural alternations, glitches in the generation process or via newly generated paths. However, stories about what people saw on the other side of those doors differ greatly, but most wanderers agree that the destination point had nothing to do with where they used to be. Therefore, some wanderers view Deleted Areas as pointers rather than barriers. These areas may indicate that Level 66 has found something worthy of deletion nearby. However, whether it concerns stopping the person from escaping, defending itself, or simply fixing mistakes made during its generational procedure remains a mystery. ----
==Wanderer Reports==
The logs and interviews below show reports of a few wanderers of '''Level 66''', and their experience within it.
Wanderer Log
''The following log is from Ezra H. Naumov, a wanderer that witnessed '''Level 66''''s generation process around them.

BEGIN LOG '''Ezra:''' I found another office. At least I thought it was another office. The room looked normal from the doorway. Cubicles, desks, lights. Same as dozens I've already passed through. I entered and immediately noticed something was wrong. The room was silent. Not empty. Silent. Level 66 is never silent. There's always distant footsteps, pipes, ventalitation, construction noises. Something. This room had nothing. I took three steps inside.x Then I heard metal scraping. It came from somewhere above me. The sound was followed by a loud impact that shook the floor. A wall appeared. {{Blurimage|file=wall111.jpg|caption=The wall.|align=right}} I don't know how else to describe it. One second there was empty space beside me. The next there was a white wall stetching from fllor to ceiling. It didn't fall into place. It simply existed. More impacts followed. The ceiling lights flickered. Another wall appeared near the far end of the room. Then a doorway. Then another. I watched a filing cabinet materialize halfway through a desk before correcting itself several seconds later. The entire room was changing around me. Building itself. The sound grew louder. Metallic crashes. Machinery. Concrete grinding against concrete. {{Blurimage|file=carpet111.jpg|caption=A section of carpeting.|align=right}} Every few seconds something new appeared. A chair. A support beam. A section of carpeting. The room wasn't finished when I entered it. Level 66 was generating it while I was standing inside. At some point the exit I'd used disappeared. Not closed. Gone. Replaced by a wall. I started moving The construction noises followed. Every hallway I entered seem incomplete. Lights appeared overhead moment before I walked in beneath them. {{Blurimage|file=generation111.jpg|caption=How is it possible?|align=right}} Doorways formed at the ends of corridors. Several rooms beyond them were little more than outlines. The farther I ran, the more unstable everything became. Then everything stopped. No noises. No movement. Nothing. I found myself standing in a completed hallway. Perfectly ordinary. As if none of it had happened. The only evidence remaining was a message scratched into the wall nearby. It read: '''"DO NOT ENTER WHILE IT IS THINKING."''' I left immediately after. END LOG
M.E.G. Interview
''The following interview was conducted by M.E.G. Researcher Keller as part of an investigation into Rendering Errors documented within '''Level 66'''.

BEGIN LOG '''Keller:''' "Can you describe the anomaly you encountered?" '''Wanderer:''' "It looked wrong." '''Keller:''' "In what way?" '''Wanderer:''' "The hallway was there, but parts of it weren't finished. The walls had this bright pattern all over them. '''Keller:''' "Similar to a checkerboard?" '''Wanderer:''' "Yeah. Exactly like that '''Keller:''' "What happened when you approached it?" '''Wanderer:''' "My eyes started hurting. '''Keller:''' Elaborate. '''Wanderer:''' "Looking directly at it felt unnatural. It wasn't just bright. My eyes couldn't focus on it. The pattern seemed flat and deep at the same time. Every time I tried to look at a specific point, it felt like it shifted somewhere else." {{Blurimage|file=missingtext111.jpg|caption=It hurts.|align=right}} '''Keller:''' "Did the anomaly affect navigation?" '''Wanderer:''' "Absolutely." '''Keller:''' "How?" '''Wanderer:''' "Because the walls stopped looking like walls. '''Keller:''' "Elaborate." '''Wanderer:''' "Normally you understand where things are. Corners. Distance. Depth. The checkerboard made everything blend together. I couldn't tell where one surface ended and another began. '''Keller:''' "You lost spatial awareness?" '''Wanderer:''' "Completely." '''Keller:''' "Was the hallway physically altered." '''Wanderer:''' "Not that I could tell. '''Keller:''' "Then why was movement difficult." {{Blurimage|file=texture222.jpg|caption=My brain couldn't process the room anymore.|align=right}} '''Wanderer:''' "Because my brain couldn't process the room anymore. '''Keller:''' "Clarify." '''Wanderer:''' "Imagine someone removed all the visual information that tells you what a structure is supposed to look like. The hallway was still there, but I couldn't understand its shape. '''Keller:''' "Did the effect persist?" '''Wanderer:''' "For a while." '''Keller:''' "How long?" '''Wanderer:''' "A few hours after I left." '''Keller:''' "Any additional observations?" '''Wanderer:''' "Yes. '''Keller:''' "Go ahead." {{Blurimage|file=texturemissing.png|caption=What do you mean?|align=right}} '''Wanderer:''' "I don't think this checkerboard pattern is an anomaly. '''Keller:''' "What do you mean?" '''Wanderer:''' "Everyone keeps saying it's a texture." '''Keller:''' "And?" '''Wanderer:''' "I think it's what's underneath the texture. '''Keller:''' "Underneath?" '''Wanderer:''' "Whatever the hallway looked like before Level 66 decided what it was supposed to be. '''Keller:''' "You believe the checkerboard is revealing an unfinished state. '''Wanderer:''' "I believe I saw the hallway before it became a hallway... END LOG
==Entities== No documented entity can be found on Level 66. Even though the level consists of huge areas and an endless number of generated environments, it has never seen an appearance of any known Backrooms Entity. What the cause of this phenomenon is still a mystery. However, the main theory is that the generation of Level 66 does not consist in generating life forms, only architecture and objects. Although the level generates various environments including fully furnished offices, warehouses and hallways, it seems unable to produce living beings. It has been observed that natural life forms are absent, including bugs, fungi and plants that grow here. {{Blurimage|file=warehouse111.jpg|caption=A photo of a warehouse but facing an area covered in pure darkness. Photographer status lost.|align=center}} Another theory suggest that the problem might not lie in the inability to generate life forms. According to the theory, Level 66 actively prevents entities from generating. The argument here is that the level can change its environment by altering, removing and replacing architectural elements. Then why would not it be able to prevent the existence of any biological beings? Whether natural or man-made, Level 66 is said to be one of the least dangerous levels in the Backrooms, where any hostile encounter is highly unlikely. But wanderers are reminded not to confuse lack of entities with lack of risk in Level 66 because of its ever-evolving layout, dangerous generated environments, Rendering Errors, and Excised Areas. While other experts might find Level 66 strange for its ability to generate rooms, some of the more curious researchers find it peculiar because it has never generated anything living even after countless generations. ==Colonies & Outposts== No colonized or outpost areas have been found within Level 66. It seems that the level creates its own separate version for each wanderer that is exploring Level 66. Consequently, wanderers are separated from each other even if they enter the level through the same point or at the same location. There have been no confirmed cases of contact between unrelated wanderers. Therefore, it is impossible to create communities. The organization of expeditions, supply points, or camps is impossible due to individuals not being able to access the same instance of the level. Items placed by one individual in their instance of the level will remain invisible to those in another version of it. While certain writings, messages, signs, or objects may be encountered within Level 66, researchers tend to associate them with previous generations of wanderers rather than the existence of a population. Due to isolation, wanderers can be sure that they are traveling alone within Level 66. ==Entrances and Exits== ===Entrances=== - Entering a room that appears unfinished or actively changing may lead to Level 66. - Passing through a door that suddenly appears where no door previously existed may lead to Level 66. - Entering an area affected by severe Rendering Errors can occasionally result in arrival within Level 66. - Walking through a door immediately after hearing metallic impacts from the opposite side may lead to Level 66. ===Exits === - Remaining inside a room while it is actively generating may No-Clip a wanderer into another level once the process concludes. - Touching a missing texture within a Rendering Error may transport a wanderer to [[Level 27.1]]. - Touching a surface while Level 66 is actively generating can result in wanderers entering [[Level 122]]. - Picking up a object moments after it materializes may lead to [[Level 20]]. ---- {{Collapse|num=999|initial='''Click for Author Information'''|final='''Close Author Information''' |body= '''Originally written by''': [[User:Arkansas Animations|Arkansas Animations]]. '''Rewriter: [[User:BewareHush|HUSH]] }} {{Redirect|Prev=65|Next=67}}

} @keyframes apartmentFade { 0% { opacity: 0; filter: blur(14px) contrast(1.3); transform: scale(1.02); } 100% { opacity: 1; filter: contrast(1.05); transform: scale(1); } } /* ========================================== MAIN ENVIRONMENT ========================================== */ .page { font-family: "Spectral", serif; color: var(--theme-page-text-color); background: radial-gradient( circle at 20% 10%, rgba(90,95,58,.25), transparent 40% ), radial-gradient( circle at 80% 80%, rgba(47,59,34,.18), transparent 45% ), linear-gradient( 180deg, #2a2f23, #353b2a ); animation: apartmentHum 18s ease-in-out infinite; } @keyframes apartmentHum { 0%,100% { filter: brightness(1); } 50% { filter: brightness(1.06); } } /* ========================================== RHOMBUS CHECKER WALLS (NOT QUITE ALIGNED) ========================================== */ .page-content::before { content: ""; position: fixed; inset: 0; pointer-events: none; background: repeating-linear-gradient( 45deg, rgba(63,74,44,.22) 0px, rgba(63,74,44,.22) 20px, rgba(90,95,58,.14) 20px, rgba(90,95,58,.14) 40px ), repeating-linear-gradient( -45deg, rgba(47,59,34,.18) 0px, rgba(47,59,34,.18) 22px, rgba(90,95,58,.10) 22px, rgba(90,95,58,.10) 44px ); opacity: .55; animation: rhombusSlide 40s linear infinite; } @keyframes rhombusSlide { from { transform: translateX(0); } to { transform: translateX(-140px); } } /* ========================================== BROWN CARPET GRID ========================================== */ .page-content::after { content: ""; position: fixed; inset: 0; pointer-events: none; background: repeating-linear-gradient( 90deg, rgba(90,60,38,.18) 0px, rgba(90,60,38,.18) 16px, transparent 16px, transparent 32px ); opacity: .65; } /* ========================================== CONTENT PANEL ========================================== */ .page__main { background: rgba(35,40,28,.6); border: 1px solid rgba(180,190,150,.12); backdrop-filter: blur(6px); box-shadow: inset 0 0 40px rgba(0,0,0,.45); } /* ========================================== HEADINGS LIKE SIGNS IN A NEVER-ENDING BUILDING ========================================== */ h1, h2, h3 { font-family: "Young Serif", serif; color: #e8e6da; text-shadow: 0 0 10px rgba(90,95,58,.2); } h4, h5, h6 { font-family: "Candal", sans-serif; color: #cfd5b6; } /* ========================================== LINKS ========================================== */ a { font-family: "Acme", sans-serif; color: var(--theme-link-color); text-decoration: none; transition: .25s ease; } a:hover { color: white; text-shadow: 0 0 10px rgba(200,220,170,.25); } /* ========================================== BLOCKS FEEL LIKE APARTMENT WALLS ========================================== */ blockquote { background: rgba(60,70,45,.22); border-left: 4px solid rgba(180,190,150,.2); padding: 1rem; border-radius: 10px; } /* ========================================== SELECTION ========================================== */ ::selection { background: rgba(140,150,110,.35);
